revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Izumo Fudoki]][[ja:出雲国風土記]][[zh:出雲國風土記]]
'''Izumo no Kuni Fudoki''' (Izumo Province 風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Compilation was ordered in May of [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 6 (713) by {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|qid=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}} and completed on the 30th day of the second month of {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|qid=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 5 (733), reportedly presented to {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|qid=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. It records various myths, including the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunibiki Myth|1=Kunibiki-shinwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420924}}, as well as other traditions unique to Izumo, differing from the accounts in the [[Japanese mythology|Kojiki and Nihon Shoki]]. It is the only extant Fudoki that survives almost in complete form.
== Compilation == In {{ill|713|en|713|qid=Q23523|lt=713|1=713}} (Wadō 6), the [[:en:Dajōkan|Dajōkan]] issued an order to compile the Fudoki. The {{ill|Kokushi|en|Kokushi|qid=Q16801292|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi}} of Izumo Province invited {{ill|Izumo no Omi Hatayasu|ja|出雲果安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Omi Hatayasu|1=Izumo no Hatayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q95161958}}, the [[Izumo no Kuni no miyatsuko|Izumo Governor]], to the Izumo [[provincial office]] and entrusted him with the task<ref name="S">{{Cite book|和書 |title=[[怪 (ムック)|怪]] |author= |date=2012 |publisher=[[Kadokawa Shoten]] |volume=35}}</ref>. By {{ill|733|en|733|qid=Q23570|lt=733|1=733}} (Tenpyō 5), under the supervision of {{ill|Izumo no Omi Hiroshima|ja|出雲広嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Omi Hiroshima|1=Izumo no Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q92924732}}, the Fudoki was compiled by {{ill|Kanyake no Omi Kanatari|ja|神宅臣金太理|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanyake no Omi Kanatari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from {{ill|Aika District|ja|秋鹿郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aika District|1=Aika district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11595889}}<ref name="S" />.
== Structure == The text is organized into a general preface, sections for each district—{{ill|Ou District|ja|意宇郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ou District|1=Ou district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11073594}}, {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11052337}}, {{ill|Aika District|ja|秋鹿郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aika District|1=Aika district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11595889}}, {{ill|Tatenui District|ja|楯縫郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatenui District|1=Tatenui district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11120028}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|1=Izumo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10896679}}, {{ill|Kando District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kando District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11591020}}, {{ill|Iishi District, Shimane|en|Iishi District, Shimane|qid=Q1349339|lt=Iishi District|1=Iishi District, Shimane}}, {{ill|Nita District, Shimane|en|Nita District, Shimane|qid=Q1204696|lt=Nita District|1=Nita District, Shimane}}, and {{ill|Ohara District, Shimane|en|Ohara District, Shimane|qid=Q865650|lt=Ohara District|1=Ohara District, Shimane}}—and a concluding section.
Each district includes a unique list of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrines|1=Shinto shrine}}, distinguishing between those registered with the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|qid=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} and those not registered.
Natural features are described alongside information on local specialties.
=== General Preface === Provides an overview of Izumo Province.
=== Ou District === For more details, refer to {{ill|Ou District|ja|意宇郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ou District|1=Ou district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11073594}}.
;Villages: Mori, Yashiro, Tatenui, Yasugi, Yamakuni, Iinashi, Toneri, Okusa, Yamashiro, Haishi, Shinjū, and Yodo. ;Post Stations: Nojo, Kuroda, and Shinjū Stations. ;Sacred Servants: Izumo Sacred Servants, Kamo Sacred Servants, Imibe Sacred Servants. ;Temples: Kyokoji and three new temples. ;Shrines: 48 registered, 19 unregistered. ;Place Names: mountains, rivers, ponds, beaches, and islands. ;Routes: roads to the national borders, Ohara District, Izumo District, and Shimane District.
=== Shimane District === For more details, refer to {{ill|Shimane District|ja|島根郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane District|1=Shimane district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11052337}}.
;Villages: Yamaguchi, Asakumi, Tedome, Miho, Katoyui, Kaga, Ikuma, Hokki, and Yodo. ;Post Stations: Senjaku Station. ;Shrines<ref group="注">There are significant omissions in the Shimane District shrine list. Supplemental lists appear in texts such as ''Izumo Fudoki Sho'' and ''Manyo-i'' (compiled by {{ill|Imai Nikan|ja|今井似閑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai Nikan|1=Imai Jikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6535865}}).</ref>: 14 registered, 45 unregistered. ;Place Names: mountains, rivers, artificial ponds, pools, ferry crossings, beaches, capes, and islands. ;Routes: roads to Ou District, Aika District, and sea crossings to {{ill|Oki Islands|en|Oki Islands|qid=Q483146|lt=Oki Islands|1=Oki Islands}}.
=== [Other Districts Section Summary Placeholder] ===
== Recorded Myths == Each village contains legends about place names, though many are fragmentary. Notable stories include: ;{{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunibiki Myth|1=Kunibiki-shinwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420924}}: Linked to the name of Ou District. ;Bime Cape Legend: The tragic story of the daughter of Katsumi-no-Imaro at Asakumi Cape. ;Kaga Divine Cape: A myth associated with Kaga's {{ill|Submarine Caves|ja|加賀の潜戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Submarine Caves|1=Kaka no kukedo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11399639}}, involving Kisa Kahi Hime.
== Deities Appearing == Several deities from the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} appear, as well as many unique to the Izumo Fudoki.
;{{ill|Yatsukamizuomitsuno|ja|八束水臣津野命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamizuomitsuno|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤美豆奴神|1=Omizunu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48745416}}: The god credited with naming Izumo and shaping its land in the Kunibiki Myth.
== Manuscripts == About 70 manuscripts survive. The oldest is believed to be the "Hosokawa Family Text" copied in {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 2 (1597).
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == * ''[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1173165/44 Fudoki],'' p.81: "Izumo no Kuni Fudoki," edited by {{ill|Yūkichi Takeda|en|Yūkichi Takeda|qid=Q2165204|lt=Takeda Sukeyoshi|1=Yūkichi Takeda}}, Iwanami Shoten (National Diet Library). * {{PDF|[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/bunkazai/kodaisen.data/handbookjou.pdf Izumo no Kuni Fudoki Handbook]}} - Shimane Prefectural Ancient Culture Center.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[de:Izumo Fudoki]]
